name: Shooter Core
summary: A playable side-scrolling shooter with free movement, bullet shooting, wave-based enemies, scoring, power-up drops, and a fail condition.
llm1Card: A complete side-scrolling shooter: move freely, shoot enemies, dodge hazards, collect power-ups, and survive waves that get harder over time.

basic.description: Side-scrolling shooter with wave-based enemies, score tracking, and power-up drops.

mobile.description: Shooter core prepared for mobile controls supplied by the Intent component bridge.

targetPlan: remove event #{{failEventIndex}} scene={{scene}}
on collision Player1 Enemy -> {{failAction}} scene={{scene}}
create scene name={{scene}} first=true
create object name=Player1 type=ShapePainter shape=rectangle color=#4488FF width=32 height=32 scene={{scene}}
create object name=Player2 type=ShapePainter shape=rectangle color=#2ECCFF width=32 height=32 scene={{scene}}
create object name=Bullet1 type=ShapePainter shape=rectangle color=#FFD700 width=12 height=6 scene={{scene}}
create object name=Bullet2 type=ShapePainter shape=rectangle color=#FFFFFF width=12 height=6 scene={{scene}}
create object name=Enemy type=ShapePainter shape=rectangle color=#DC3232 width=28 height=28 scene={{scene}}
create object name=PowerUp type=ShapePainter shape=circle color=#2ECC71 width=16 height=16 scene={{scene}}
create object name=HudScore type=Text size=20 scene={{scene}}
create object name=HudWave type=Text size=20 scene={{scene}}
set variable name=Score value=0 type=Number scope=global
set variable name=Wave value=1 type=Number scope=global
set variable name=Player1Health value=3 type=Number scope=global
set variable name=Player2Health value=3 type=Number scope=global
place object=Player1 at=100,250 scene={{scene}}
place object=Player2 at=100,350 scene={{scene}}
place object=HudScore at=16,16 scene={{scene}}
place object=HudWave at=16,44 scene={{scene}}
on start -> Score=0, Wave=1, Player1Health=3, Player2Health=3 scene={{scene}}
on start -> text HudScore Score scene={{scene}}
on start -> text HudWave Wave scene={{scene}}
on key ArrowUp held -> move Player1 y=-4 scene={{scene}}
on key ArrowDown held -> move Player1 y=+4 scene={{scene}}
on key ArrowLeft held -> move Player1 x=-4 scene={{scene}}
on key ArrowRight held -> move Player1 x=+4 scene={{scene}}
on key Space -> spawn Bullet1 at 120 260 scene={{scene}}
on key KeyW held -> move Player2 y=-4 scene={{scene}}
on key KeyS held -> move Player2 y=+4 scene={{scene}}
on key KeyA held -> move Player2 x=-4 scene={{scene}}
on key KeyD held -> move Player2 x=+4 scene={{scene}}
on key KeyF -> spawn Bullet2 at 120 360 scene={{scene}}
on collision Bullet1 Enemy -> destroy Bullet1, destroy Enemy, score+10 scene={{scene}}
on collision Bullet2 Enemy -> destroy Bullet2, destroy Enemy, score+10 scene={{scene}}
on collision Player1 PowerUp -> destroy PowerUp, variable Player1Health = 3 scene={{scene}}
on collision Player2 PowerUp -> destroy PowerUp, variable Player2Health = 3 scene={{scene}}
on collision Player1 Enemy -> {{failAction}} scene={{scene}}
on collision Player2 Enemy -> {{failAction}} scene={{scene}}
every 2s -> spawn Enemy at 780 100 scene={{scene}}
every 2s -> spawn Enemy at 780 250 scene={{scene}}
every 3s -> spawn Enemy at 780 400 scene={{scene}}
every 15s -> spawn PowerUp at 400 300 scene={{scene}}

capabilities.name: Base scene
capabilities.summary: Create the playable scene and base canvas for a GameCastle project.
capabilities.llm1Hint: A game can have one main playable scene on an 800x600 canvas.

constraints.sceneName: Use Game unless the user explicitly asks for another scene.

capabilities.name: Shape objects and text
capabilities.summary: Create visible game objects using ShapePainter rectangles/circles and Text objects.
capabilities.llm1Hint: Visuals use simple colored shapes and text, enough for players, enemies, bullets, power-ups, and HUD labels.

constraints.objectNames: Use stable English names. Do not rename objects unless explicitly requested.
constraints.shapePainter: ShapePainter objects must include shape, color, width, and height.

capabilities.name: Free 2D movement
capabilities.summary: Move an object freely in all four directions via held keys, without gravity or platform physics.
capabilities.llm1Hint: The player can move freely up, down, left, and right through the game world.

constraints.speed: Default move speed is 4px per frame. Bullet speed is 8px per tick.
constraints.boundary: Constrain the player inside the 800x600 canvas to prevent escaping the play area.
constraints.noGravity: Free 2D objects do NOT use PlatformBehavior. Movement is purely event-driven via move_rel (MettreXY with +/- operators).

capabilities.name: Projectile shooting
capabilities.summary: Spawn a projectile on key press that travels in a direction and destroys on enemy collision.
capabilities.llm1Hint: The player can shoot bullets that travel across the screen and destroy enemies.

constraints.spawnOffset: Spawn bullets near the player. Concrete spawn offsets are runtime/placement facts owned by the compiler and bridge.
constraints.bulletSpeed: Bullet speed is a semantic tuning value. The compiler/runtime bridge owns tick intervals and movement deltas.
constraints.cooldown: Fire-rate cooldown is a gameplay pacing intent. Runtime/compiler owners should implement timers or variable gates.
constraints.movement: Bullet movement uses the move_rel action (GDevelop MettreXY with +/- operators) on a repeating timer.

capabilities.name: Wave-based enemy spawning
capabilities.summary: Spawn enemies in timed waves from off-screen. Supports random Y position and per-wave difficulty ramp.
capabilities.llm1Hint: Enemies appear in waves from the right side of the screen. Each wave is harder than the last.

constraints.spawnSide: Enemies spawn from the front/right side of the play space with varied lanes. Exact edge positions are placement-resolver facts.
constraints.waveInterval: Wave cadence is a semantic difficulty value. The compiler/runtime bridge owns timer expansion and lane variety.
constraints.enemySpeed: Enemies advance against player progression. Concrete movement deltas and event expansion belong to the bridge/runtime owners.
constraints.ramping: Difficulty ramping is expressed as higher pressure or denser waves; compiler/runtime owners expand it into counters, timers, or spawn plans.

capabilities.name: Power-up drops
capabilities.summary: Enemies may drop power-ups on death. Power-ups grant temporary or permanent benefits on player collection.
capabilities.llm1Hint: Destroyed enemies sometimes leave behind power-ups that heal or strengthen the player.

constraints.dropInterval: Default power-up spawn interval is 15s. Keep it infrequent so power-ups feel rewarding.
constraints.effectOptions: Current preset supports health restore. New effects such as speed boost or score multiplier should enter as semantic component/module capabilities.
constraints.cleanup: Power-ups that leave the play space should be cleaned up by runtime-owned bounds or lifetime rules.
constraints.spawn: Power-up spawn placement is semantic, such as near defeated enemies or safe player routes; exact positions and variation are resolver/runtime facts.
